1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
153

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. 45	Mathlib's `pullback.lift_fst` / `pullback.lift_snd` are `@[reassoc]` ONLY, not
46	`@[simp]`, so the abbrev `pullback.map _ _ _ _ i₁ i₂ i₃ _ _ ≫ pullback.fst _ _`
47	is not collapsed by `simp` even though the equation holds definitionally. These
48	two local simp lemmas unblock projection-chain collapse for
49	`gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` whose STEP 4 uses
50	`asIso (pullback.map …)`. Candidate for upstream Mathlib contribution. -/
51	
52	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
53	lemma pullback_map_fst_proj {C : Type*} [Category C] {W X Y Z S T : C}
54	    (f₁ : W ⟶ S) (f₂ : X ⟶ S) [Limits.HasPullback f₁ f₂] (g₁ : Y ⟶ T)
55	    (g₂ : Z ⟶ T) [Limits.HasPullback g₁ g₂] (i₁ : W ⟶ Y) (i₂ : X ⟶ Z) (i₃ : S ⟶ T)
56	    (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ i₃ = i₁ ≫ g₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ i₃ = i₂ ≫ g₂) :
57	    Limits.pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂ ≫
58	        Limits.pullback.fst g₁ g₂ =
59	      Limits.pullback.fst f₁ f₂ ≫ i₁ :=
60	  Limits.pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
61	
62	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
63	lemma pullback_map_snd_proj {C : Type*} [Category C] {W X Y Z S T : C}
64	    (f₁ : W ⟶ S) (f₂ : X ⟶ S) [Limits.HasPullback f₁ f₂] (g₁ : Y ⟶ T)
65	    (g₂ : Z ⟶ T) [Limits.HasPullback g₁ g₂] (i₁ : W ⟶ Y) (i₂ : X ⟶ Z) (i₃ : S ⟶ T)
66	    (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ i₃ = i₁ ≫ g₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ i₃ = i₂ ≫ g₂) :
67	    Limits.pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂ ≫
68	        Limits.pullback.snd g₁ g₂ =
69	      Limits.pullback.snd f₁ f₂ ≫ i₂ :=
70	  Limits.pullback.lift_snd _ _ _

72	/-! ### Chart-bridge: `Proj.awayι ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away _ _))`
73	
74	The helper below is the iter-173 `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe step (a)
75	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). Used by `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (below the `gmScalingP1_cover`
76	definition). -/
77	
78	/-- **`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f _ _ ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 f))`** for any
79	homogeneous element `f` of positive degree.
80	
81	Generic in the element `f` AND in the degree `m` so we can apply it to either
82	`(![X 0, X 1]) i` (the actual chart input from `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover.f i`,
83	which uses `m = (![1, 1]) i`) or `X i` (which uses `m = 1`). A pure rewrite chasing
84	`awayι_toSpecZero` + `Spec.map_comp` + the `algebraKbarAway` defeq. -/
85	private lemma awayι_comp_PLB_hom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
86	    {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)
87	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar m) :
88	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f hf hm ≫
89	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
90	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar
91	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f))) := by
92	  change Proj.awayι _ _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
93	  rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.awayι_toSpecZero, ← Spec.map_comp,
94	    ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
95	  rfl
96	
97	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
98	
99	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
100	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
101	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
102	
103	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
104	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
105	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
106	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
107	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
108	
109	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
110	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
111	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
112	
113	/-- **Chart-1 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
114	coord `u = X 0 / X 1 ↦ u ⊗ λ`, where `λ = X () ∈ GmRing kbar`. Uses
115	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` with the algebra-map `kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing`
116	(target carrier carries `Algebra kbar` because both factors do). Axiom-clean. -/
117	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
118	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
119	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
120	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
121	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))
122	
123	/-- **Chart-0 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
124	coord `t = X 1 / X 0 ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The `λ⁻¹` is `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf (X ())` in
125	`GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`. Axiom-clean. -/
126	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
127	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
128	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
129	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
130	      (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
131	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :
132	        GmRing kbar))
133	
134	/-- **The pullback open cover of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`** along `pullback.fst`, indexed by
135	the 2-chart cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` of `ProjectiveLineBar.left`. The `i`-th
136	component is `pullback (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) (Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X i) …)`. -/
137	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
138	    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left.OpenCover :=
139	  (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.pullback₁
140	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
141	
142	/-- **The chart-`i` source of `gmScalingP1_cover` is
143	`Spec ((Away 𝒜 ((![X 0, X 1]) i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`.**
144	
145	Built by composing `pullbackSymmetry`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`, the
146	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` rewrite via `pullback.congrHom`, and `pullbackSpecIso`. Mirrors the
147	Mathlib precedent `OpenCover.pullbackCoverAffineRefinementObjIso`
148	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open:160-166`). Used by `gmScalingP1_chart`.
149	
150	**Uniform-in-`i` (iter-179 refactor `cover-bridge-uniform-i`):** the target type carries
151	`((![X 0, X 1]) i)` rather than `MvPolynomial.X i`. The hoisted helpers
152	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`/`_hm` together with the m-generalised
153	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` keep the bridge chain syntactic so `pullbackSpecIso` applies
154	generically — no `match`-on-`i` decoration. -/
155	private noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
156	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ≅
157	      Spec (CommRingCat.of
158	        (TensorProduct kbar
159	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
160	            ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
161	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
162	          (GmRing kbar))) :=
163	  pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
164	    pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
165	    pullback.congrHom
166	      (awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm i)
167	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
168	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)
169	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i))
170	      (show (Gm kbar).hom =
171	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) from rfl) ≪≫
172	    pullbackSpecIso kbar _ (GmRing kbar)
173	
174	/-- **The chart-`i` scheme morphism** `(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme`
175	defining `σ_×` on the `i`-th chart. On chart-1 (target `D₊(X 1)`), the affine coord
176	`u = X 0 / X 1` is sent to `u ⊗ λ`; on chart-0 (target `D₊(X 0)`), `t = X 1 / X 0` is sent
177	to `t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The scheme map is built from `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` (the chart-side
178	ring maps) via `pullbackSpecIso` + (the chart-ring iso
179	`HomogeneousLocalization.Away ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar`) + `Proj.awayι`.
180	
181	**Status (iter-173):** body landed via the `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe
182	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). The bridge `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (above) identifies the
183	source with `Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing)`. The chart-ring iso
184	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` plus a chart-`i`-specific `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` produces
185	the ring map `Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* Away 𝒜 (X i) ⊗ GmRing`, then `Proj.awayι` lands the
186	result in `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`. -/
187	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
188	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar :=
189	  (gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar i).hom ≫
190	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
191	      ((MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
192	          (fun _ : Unit =>
193	            (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem
194	                (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i)
195	                (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar (otherFin i))) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
196	              (match i with
197	               | ⟨0, _⟩ =>
198	                  (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
199	                    (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) : GmRing kbar)
200	               | ⟨1, _⟩ =>
201	                  algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)
202	                    (MvPolynomial.X ())))).comp
203	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom)) ≫
204	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
205	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
206	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) Nat.one_pos

208	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
209	/-- **Shared per-chart helper for `gmScalingP1`** (iter-174 Sub-task A per
210	`analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3). On the `i`-th chart of the
211	`gmScalingP1_cover`, the composition `gmScalingP1_chart kbar i ≫ PLB.hom` agrees with
212	`(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom`.
213	
214	This is the per-chart certificate used by `gmScalingP1_over_coherence` (via
215	`Scheme.Cover.hom_ext` + `Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms_assoc`).
216	
217	**Status (iter-180):** body retired axiom-clean via the empirically-verified
218	`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` recipe of
219	`analogies/pullbackspeciso-bypass.md` (Decision 4). The option collapses the
220	`Algebra.compHom`-driven heartbeat sink on the `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` rewrite
221	that blocked iter-175 through iter-179. -/
222	private lemma gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
223	    gmScalingP1_chart kbar i ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
224	      (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by
225	  -- Stage 1 (iter-179): unfold the chart map, apply `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` after a
226	  -- type-realigning `change`, and collapse the right-hand `Spec.map` chain into
227	  -- `Spec.map (algMap kbar (Away_i ⊗ GmRing))` via
228	  -- `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap` + `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_comp_C`.
229	  unfold gmScalingP1_chart
230	  have h := awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (m := 1) Nat.one_pos
231	    (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
232	    (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
233	  change (gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar i).hom ≫ _ ≫
234	      ((Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
235	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
236	          (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) Nat.one_pos :
237	        Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
238	          (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (MvPolynomial.X i))) ⟶
239	          Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)) ≫
240	        (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom) = _
241	  rw [h, ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
242	    homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq,
243	    MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_comp_C]
244	  -- Stage 2 (iter-180 fix): the `respectTransparency` option lets the
245	  -- `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` simp lemma fire on the buried iso chain;
246	  -- it produces `pullback.fst _ _ ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar Away_i)` in place
247	  -- of `(pullbackSpecIso).hom ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar (Away_i ⊗ GmRing))`.
248	  -- The follow-up simps collapse the `pullback.congrHom`-wrapped map into a
249	  -- bare `pullback.fst (cover.f i ≫ PLB.hom) Gm.hom`.
250	  unfold gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso
251	  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Category.assoc, pullbackSpecIso_hom_base,
252	    pullback.congrHom_hom, pullback.lift_fst_assoc, Category.id_comp]
253	  -- Stage 3: re-align `Proj.awayι (![X 0, X 1] i) ⋯ ⋯` with `(cover.openCover.f i)`
254	  -- so `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_hom_fst_assoc` can match its `f' ≫ f` pattern.
255	  change (pullbackSymmetry (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
256	        ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i)).hom ≫
257	      (pullbackRightPullbackFstIso (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom
258	          ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i)).hom ≫
259	        pullback.fst
260	            ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i ≫
261	              (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom)
262	            (Gm kbar).hom ≫
263	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar
264	            (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
265	              ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
266	                Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)))) =
267	        (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom
268	  simp only [pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_hom_fst_assoc,
269	    pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst_assoc]
270	  -- Stage 4: reverse the chart bridge back to `cover.f i ≫ PLB.hom` so the residual
271	  -- matches `(PLB ⊗ Gm).hom`'s definitional form.
272	  rw [← awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm i)
273	    ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
274	      Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)
275	    (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i)]
276	  -- Stage 5: expose `(PLB ⊗ Gm).hom = pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom ≫ PLB.hom`
277	  -- and `(gmScalingP1_cover).f i = pullback.fst (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom)
278	  -- ((cover).openCover.f i)`, then close via `pullback.condition_assoc`.
279	  change pullback.snd (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
280	        ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i) ≫
281	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i ≫
282	        (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
283	    pullback.fst (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
284	        ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i) ≫
285	      pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom ≫
286	        (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom
287	  rw [← pullback.condition_assoc]

289	/-- **Intersection-cover X iso** (iter-182 Lane B helper per
290	`analogies/intersection-ring-cross01.md` Decision 3 Recipe 1).
291	
292	Identifies the pullback over the cross chart `(cover.f 0) ⨯ (cover.f 1)` with
293	`Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X 0 * X 1)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing)`, mirroring `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`
294	with the merged generator `X 0 * X 1` of degree 2.
295	
296	The body is a chain of iso steps:
297	1. `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso q awayι_1 (cover.f 0)`: paste the outer pullback
298	   into a left-side composition `pullback (cover.f 0 ≫ q) awayι_1`.
299	2. `pullback.congrHom pullback.condition rfl`: rewrite `cover.f 0 ≫ q` as
300	   `pullback.snd q awayι_0 ≫ awayι_0`.
301	3. `(pullbackRightPullbackFstIso awayι_0 awayι_1 (pullback.snd q awayι_0)).symm`:
302	   bring the outer pullback into `pullback (pullback.snd q awayι_0) (pullback.fst awayι_0 awayι_1)`.
303	4. `pullback.map` with `i₂ = (Proj.pullbackAwayιIso ...).hom`: replace the inner
304	   pullback `pullback awayι_0 awayι_1 ≅ Spec (Away (X 0 * X 1))` (via
305	   `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso`), with the new "fst" being
306	   `Spec.map (awayMap (X 1 hom) rfl)` (via `pullbackAwayιIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_left`).
307	5. `pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso q awayι_0 (Spec.map (awayMap _ _))`: collapse the
308	   outer pullback to `pullback q (Spec.map (awayMap _ _) ≫ awayι_0)`.
309	6. `pullback.congrHom rfl (Proj.SpecMap_awayMap_awayι ...)`: identify
310	   `Spec.map (awayMap _ _) ≫ awayι_0 = awayι_(X_0 * X_1)`.
311	7. Apply the standard recipe (mirror of `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`) at the merged
312	   generator `X 0 * X 1` of degree 2: `pullbackSymmetry`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`,
313	   `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` (m = 2), `pullbackSpecIso`. -/
314	private noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso
315	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
316	    pullback ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (0 : Fin 2))
317	        ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (1 : Fin 2)) ≅
318	      Spec (CommRingCat.of
319	        (TensorProduct kbar
320	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
321	            ((MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) * MvPolynomial.X 1))
322	          (GmRing kbar))) :=
323	  -- **iter-186 Lane B path (III.a): refactor to term-mode `Iso.trans`-spine**.
324	  -- This single-chain term-mode definition exposes the iso's `≪≫`-spine
325	  -- syntactically so the Mathlib `Iso.trans_inv`-based simp chain can unfold
326	  -- it link-by-link. Functionally equivalent to the prior tactic-mode form
327	  -- (`refine ≪≫ ?_; refine ≪≫ ?_; ...`); only the elaboration shape changes.
328	  pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
329	    pullback.congrHom pullback.condition rfl ≪≫
330	    (pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _).symm ≪≫
331	    asIso (pullback.map _ _ _ _ (𝟙 _)
332	      (Proj.pullbackAwayιIso (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
333	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 0)
334	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 0)
335	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)
336	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1)
337	        rfl).hom (𝟙 _)
338	      (by rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp])
339	      (by
340	        rw [Category.comp_id]
341	        exact (Proj.pullbackAwayιIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_left
342	          (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) _ _ _ _ _).symm)) ≪≫
343	    pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso _ _ _ ≪≫
344	    pullback.congrHom rfl
345	      (Proj.SpecMap_awayMap_awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
346	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 0)
347	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 0)
348	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1) rfl) ≪≫
349	    pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
350	    pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
351	    pullback.congrHom
352	      (awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (m := 2) (by norm_num)
353	        ((MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) * MvPolynomial.X 1)
354	        ((MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 0).mul (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1)))
355	      (show (Gm kbar).hom =
356	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) from rfl) ≪≫
357	    pullbackSpecIso kbar _ (GmRing kbar)

359	/-- **The substantive `(0, 1)` cross case of `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`** (iter-181

362	This is the *single* non-trivial ring-level identity for the cocycle:
363	
364	`pullback.fst ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 0 =
365	   pullback.snd ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 1`.
366	
367	On the basic-open intersection `D₊(X 0 · X 1) ⊆ ProjectiveLineBarScheme` both chart
368	coordinates are units (chart-0 coord `t = X 1 / X 0`, chart-1 coord `u = X 0 / X 1`,
369	with `t · u = 1`). Under `σ_×([X 0 : X 1], λ) = [λ · X 0 : X 1]` the two chart maps
370	satisfy on the intersection:
371	
372	* chart-0: `t ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹` (i.e. `t/λ`)
373	* chart-1: `u ↦ u ⊗ λ` (i.e. `λ · u`)
374	
375	Substituting `u = 1/t` on the chart-1 side gives `λ · u = λ · (1/t) = λ/t`, and on the
376	chart-0 side `t/λ` corresponds to `u' = 1/(t/λ) = λ/t` under the `u ↔ 1/t` change of
377	coords. Hence the ring-level identity is `λ · u = (1/t) · λ` in
378	`Localization.Away t ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar`, which after multiplying both sides by `t`
379	reduces to `λ · u · t = λ`, true because `u · t = 1`.

1586	      The chart-agreement equation
1587	      \(\mathrm{pullback.fst} \fatsemi \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,\bar k\,0 =
1588	      \mathrm{pullback.snd} \fatsemi \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,\bar k\,1\)
1589	      on the cover intersection
1590	      \(\mathrm{pullback}\,((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_0.f)\,
1591	      ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_1.f)\) is the statement that two morphisms
1592	      \(\mathrm{chart}_0, \mathrm{chart}_1 \colon \mathrm{(intersection)} \to
1593	      \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) agree on that intersection scheme. \emph{The key
1594	      observation is that \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\) is separated over \(\bar k\)},
1595	      so the diagonal
1596	      \(\Delta_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}} \colon
1597	        \overline{\mathbb P^1} \longrightarrow
1598	        \overline{\mathbb P^1} \times_{\bar k} \overline{\mathbb P^1}\)
1599	      is a closed immersion. Because \(\overline{\mathbb P^1} = \Proj(\bar k[X_0, X_1])\)
1600	      is the \(\Proj\) of a graded \(\bar k\)-algebra, its separatedness over \(\Spec
1601	      \bar k\) is the standard Stacks fact ``\(\Proj(S)\) is separated'' (Stacks tag
1602	      \texttt{01KU} / lemma \texttt{lemma-proj-separated}); Mathlib supplies the
1603	      categorical packaging via \(\mathtt{IsSeparated}\) and
1604	      \(\mathtt{IsSeparated.diagonal\_isClosedImmersion}\).
1605	      \emph{Bypass.} Reformulate the cocycle as: the pair-morphism
1606	      \((\mathrm{chart}_0, \mathrm{chart}_1) \colon \mathrm{(intersection)}
1607	      \longrightarrow \overline{\mathbb P^1} \times_{\bar k} \overline{\mathbb P^1}\)
1608	      (built via \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Limits.prod.lift}\) on
1609	      \(\mathrm{chart}_0 := \mathrm{pullback.fst} \fatsemi \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,
1610	      \bar k\,0\) and
1611	      \(\mathrm{chart}_1 := \mathrm{pullback.snd} \fatsemi \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,
1612	      \bar k\,1\)) \emph{factors through the diagonal
1613	      \(\Delta_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}}\)}. By Mathlib's
1614	      \(\mathtt{IsClosedImmersion.lift\_iff\_range\_subset}\) (the universal property
1615	      of a closed immersion: a morphism factors through a closed immersion iff its
1616	      set-theoretic image is contained in the image of the immersion), the
1617	      factorization is equivalent to the set-level containment of the cocycle's image
1618	      in the image of \(\Delta\), which on points is exactly the chart equality on
1619	      the \emph{closed points} of the intersection. The closed-point check is
1620	      discrete (each closed point of the intersection lies over a single \(\bar k\)-
1621	      point of \(D_+(X_0 \cdot X_1) \times \mathbb G_m\) and the two chart maps both
1622	      evaluate to the same \(\bar k\)-point of \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\)
1623	      corresponding to that \((x, \lambda)\)), and bypasses the simp coverage gap
1624	      that obstructs (III.a)/(III.b) entirely.

1626	      \begin{enumerate}

1630	            \overline{\mathbb P^1}\)
1631	          via Mathlib's \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Limits.prod.lift}\,(\mathbf
1632	          1_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}})\,(\mathbf 1_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}})\), and
1633	          obtain the closed-immersion witness from
1634	          \(\mathtt{IsSeparated.diagonal\_isClosedImmersion}\) applied to the
1635	          separated structure morphism
1636	          \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}.\mathrm{hom} \colon \overline{\mathbb P^1} \to
1637	          \Spec \bar k\).
1638	        \item Build the pair-morphism
1639	          \((\mathrm{chart}_0, \mathrm{chart}_1) \colon
1640	            \mathrm{pullback}\,((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_0.f)\,
1641	              ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_1.f)
1642	            \longrightarrow

1644	          via \(\mathtt{prod.lift}\) on the two named chart restrictions above.
1645	        \item Show this pair factors through \(\Delta_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}}\)
1646	          via the universal property of the intersection's pullback structure
1647	          combined with \(\mathtt{IsClosedImmersion.lift}\) (kernel-inequality
1648	          form; the \(\mathtt{\_iff\_range\_subset}\) variant is NOT in Mathlib
1649	          at \texttt{b80f227} per iter-188 verification, so this step requires
1650	          either upstream contribution or a project-side bridge plus
1651	          \(\mathtt{IsReduced}\) on the intersection): produce the section
1652	          \(\mathrm{(intersection)} \to \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) whose post-
1653	          composition with \(\Delta\) equals \((\mathrm{chart}_0,
1654	          \mathrm{chart}_1)\).
1655	        \item The factorization reduces, by composing with the two projections
1656	          \(\mathtt{prod.fst}\) and \(\mathtt{prod.snd}\) out of the codomain
1657	          \(\overline{\mathbb P^1} \times_{\bar k} \overline{\mathbb P^1}\), to
1658	          two scheme-equalities on the components --- each of which is a direct
1659	          chart-bridge equality on \((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_i\)'s structure

1661	          using the same chart-ring iso \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} and
1662	          \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\) bridge that \cref{def:gmscaling_chart} uses.
1663	          The chart-\(1\) instance of the per-component equality threads
1664	          through the named \(\mathrm{Proj.appIso}\) evaluation
1665	          \cref{lem:iotaGm_chart1_appIso_eval}, which packages the basic-open
1666	          ring-map identification
1667	          \(\mathtt{isLocElem} \mapsto [X_0/X_1] \mapsto 1\) at the global-sections
1668	          level so the prover can target it directly without re-deriving the
1669	          \((\mathrm{Proj.appIso}).\mathrm{inv}\) image of \(\mathtt{isLocElem}\)
1670	          inline (the iter-188 through iter-191 budget-wall step). The chart-\(0\)
1671	          instance is the symmetric statement with roles of \(X_0, X_1\)
1672	          interchanged.
1673	      \end{enumerate}
1674	      \emph{Substrate hooks.} The categorical substrate is present at the
1675	      pinned Mathlib commit \texttt{b80f227}:
1676	      \(\mathtt{IsSeparated.diagonal\_isClosedImmersion}\),
1677	      \(\mathtt{IsClosedImmersion.lift}\) (kernel-inequality form),
1678	      \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Limits.prod.lift}\),
1679	      \(\mathtt{pullback.lift}\), together with the standard
1680	      \(\mathtt{IsSeparated}\) instance for \(\Proj\) of a graded ring (Stacks
1681	      \texttt{01KU} / Hartshorne~II.4.6 diagonal-is-closed characterisation).
1682	      % NOTE (iter-188 review): the \texttt{\_iff\_range\_subset} variant of
1683	      % \texttt{IsClosedImmersion.lift} cited in the (III.c) recipe is NOT present
1684	      % at b80f227 (verified iter-188 via lean\_leansearch on
1685	      % \enquote{morphism factors through closed immersion iff range subset}
1686	      % and \enquote{IsClosedImmersion lift range subset}; both return only the
1687	      % kernel-inequality form). Closure requires either a Mathlib upstream
1688	      % contribution of \texttt{IsClosedImmersion.lift\_iff\_range\_subset} or a
1689	      % project-side build that also threads
1690	      % \texttt{Algebra.TensorProduct.isDomain\_of\_isAlgClosed\_left}-style
1691	      % reducedness of the intersection. See
1692	      % \texttt{.archon/task\_results/AlgebraicJacobian\_Genus0BaseObjects\_GmScaling.lean.md}
1693	      % \S\enquote{Mathlib lemmas confirmed NOT SHIPPED}.
1694	      \emph{Cost estimate (post iter-188 falsification).} \(\sim 150\)--\(200\)
1695	      LOC over 3--5 iters (Option B: project-side substrate including
1696	      \(\mathtt{lift\_iff\_range\_subset}\) plus \(\mathtt{IsReduced}\) on the
1697	      intersection); Option A (Mathlib upstream) is unbounded in time.
1698	  \end{description}
1699	  \medskip
1700	  \noindent\textbf{(IV) Substantive ring-level residual.} All three pickup paths reduce,
1701	  after their structural setup, to the same ring-level identity. On the basic-open
1702	  intersection \(D_+(X_0 \cdot X_1) \subseteq \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) both chart
1703	  coordinates are units (the chart-\(0\) coordinate \(t = X_1/X_0\) and the chart-\(1\)
1704	  coordinate \(u = X_0/X_1\) are reciprocals: \(t \cdot u = 1\)), and the agreement
1705	  reduces to the ring-level identity
1706	  \[ \lambda \cdot u \ =\ \frac{1}{t} \cdot \lambda \quad
1707	     \text{in } \mathrm{Localization.Away}\,t \otimes_{\bar k} \mathtt{GmRing}\,\bar k, \]
1708	  i.e.\ to the unit-of-the-localisation identity \(t \cdot u = 1\)
1709	  (\(\mathtt{IsLocalization.Away.mul\_invSelf}\)) together with commutativity of the
1710	  tensor product. The proof routes through the chart-ring iso
1711	  \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} and the same \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\) bridge that
1712	  \cref{def:gmscaling_chart} uses; consequently \cref{lem:gmscaling_chart_agreement}
1713	  remains gated on \cref{def:gmscaling_chart} acquiring its concrete body \emph{and} on
1714	  one of the (III.a)--(III.c) structural setups landing. See
1715	  \(\mathtt{analogies/gmscaling{\text-}projection{\text-}idiom.md}\) (iter-184) for the
1716	  full Recipe~1/2/3 decomposition and empirical simp-probe verification.
1717	\end{lemma}
1718	  
1719	\begin{lemma}
1720	\leanok
1721	[Per-chart bridge \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,i\,\fatsemi\,\mathbb P^1.\mathrm{hom}\)]
1722	  \label{lem:gmscaling_chart_PLB_eq}
1723	  
1724	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq}
1725	  % Archon-original Lean encoding; the per-chart certificate that
1726	  % \cref{lem:gmscaling_over_coherence} consumes after \(\mathtt{Scheme.Cover.hom\_ext}\) and
1727	  % \(\mathtt{ι\_glueMorphisms\_assoc}\) reduce the over-side coherence to per-chart equations.
1728	  % No external % SOURCE QUOTE: this is bespoke to the project's iter-171 chart-glue split of
1729	  % \(\sigma_\times = \mathtt{gmScalingP1}\).
1730	  For each chart index \(i \in \{0, 1\} = \mathrm{Fin}\,2\), the chart-\(i\) scheme morphism
1731	  \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,\bar k\,i\) (\cref{def:gmscaling_chart}) intertwines the
1732	  structure morphism of the cover chart \((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover}\,\bar k).f\,i\)
1733	  (\cref{def:gmscaling_cover}) with the structure morphism of \(\mathbb P^1\):
1734	  \[ \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,\bar k\,i
1735	     \,\fatsemi\,
1736	     \mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k.\mathrm{hom}
1737	     \ =\
1738	     (\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover}\,\bar k).f\,i
1739	     \,\fatsemi\,
1740	     \bigl((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k) \otimes \mathtt{Gm}\,\bar k\bigr).\mathrm{hom}. \]
1741	  This is the per-chart certificate that, after \(\mathtt{Scheme.Cover.hom\_ext}\) followed by
1742	  \(\mathtt{ι\_glueMorphisms\_assoc}\), closes the over-side coherence
1743	  \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_over\_coherence}\) (\cref{lem:gmscaling_over_coherence}) by which the
1744	  glued total morphism \(\sigma_\times = \mathtt{gmScalingP1}\) (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1}) is
1745	  promoted from a bare \(\mathbf{Sch}\)-level map to a morphism in \(\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)\).
1746	  \medskip
1747	  \noindent\textbf{Three-step reduction.} The proof has three steps. All three route through
1748	  the project-side chart-bridge \(\mathtt{awayι\_comp\_PLB\_hom}\)
1749	  (\(\mathtt{Genus0BaseObjects.lean}\), post-iter-175 split moves to
1750	  \(\mathtt{Genus0BaseObjects/ChartBridge.lean}\)), which identifies $\mathrm{Proj.awayι}
1751	  \,\fatsemi\, \mathbb P^1.\mathrm{hom}\( with \)\Spec.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathrm{algebraMap}\,
1752	  \bar k\,(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i))\( for the degree-\)1$ homogeneous generators
1753	  \(X_i\).
1754	  \begin{description}
1755	    \item[(A) Collapse \(\mathrm{Proj.awayι} \fatsemi \mathbb P^1.\mathrm{hom}\).] Apply the
1756	      chart-bridge \(\mathtt{awayι\_comp\_PLB\_hom}\) to rewrite the tail of the chart-\(i\)
1757	      morphism, replacing \(\mathrm{Proj.awayι} \,\fatsemi\, \mathbb P^1.\mathrm{hom}\) with
1758	      \(\Spec.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathrm{algebraMap}\,\bar k\,(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i))\).
1759	    \item[(B) Merge the \(\Spec.\mathrm{map}\)'s.] Use \(\Spec.\mathrm{map\_comp}\) to merge the
1760	      two consecutive \(\Spec.\mathrm{map}\)'s on the chart-\(i\) side, then apply the
1761	      chart-ring iso's \(\bar k\)-algebra preservation
1762	      (\cref{lem:chart_ring_iso_preserves_algebraMap}) together with
1763	      \(\mathrm{MvPolynomial.eval}_2\mathrm{Hom}\_C\) to identify the merged ring map with
1764	      $\mathrm{algebraMap}\,\bar k\,(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i \otimes_{\bar k}
1765	      \mathtt{GmRing}\,\bar k)$.

1767	      \(\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover\_X\_iso}\,\bar k\,i\) (identifying the cover chart with
1768	      \(\Spec\) of an \(\bar k\)-tensor product of chart-\(i\) and \(\mathtt{GmRing}\)). Then use
1769	      Mathlib's \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso\_hom\_base}\),
1770	      \(\mathtt{pullbackRightPullbackFstIso\_hom\_fst}\), and
1771	      \(\mathtt{pullbackSymmetry\_hom\_comp\_fst}\) to chase the source side back to
1772	      \((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover}\,\bar k).f\,i\) composed with the structure morphism of
1773	      the relative tensor product. The final identification with the RHS is via Mathlib's
1774	      \(\mathtt{Over.tensorObj\_hom}\) and \(\mathtt{pullback.condition}\).
1775	  \end{description}

10	/-!
11	# Cross01 substrate — Lane B (𝔾ₘ-scaling) reusable lemmas
12	
13	This file ships **substrate** lemmas needed to close the Lane B
14	`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01` cocycle proof (path (III.c) of
15	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`) together with two off-target sorries
16	(`gm_geomIrred`, `projGm_isReduced`) in `Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean`.
17	
18	The substrates are generic in the geometric situation and live in their own
19	file rather than inline so they can be re-used by the three GmScaling consumer
20	applications and, if upstreamed, by Mathlib itself.  Their construction
21	follows the recipe of `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` (§2) and the prover-facing
22	specification at `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`.
23	
24	## Contents (this iter)
25	
26	* `AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` —
27	  Substrate 1 (iter-189, axiom-clean): the closed-immersion universal
28	  property phrased as a topological range containment (under reduced
29	  source / target hypotheses).
30	
31	* `AlgebraicGeometry.gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain` —
32	  Substrate 2 (iter-190): the tensor
33	  `Away f ⊗_{k̄} GmRing k̄` is a domain, via the iso chain to
34	  `Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit (Away f))` per
35	  `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` §3.

38	set_option autoImplicit false
39	set_option linter.style.setOption false
40	set_option linter.style.show false
41	set_option linter.unusedSimpArgs false
42	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000
43	
44	universe u

46	open CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace Topology

48	noncomputable section

50	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
51	
52	namespace IsClosedImmersion
53	
54	/--
55	**Closed immersion lift by range inclusion.**
56	For a closed immersion `i : Z ⟶ X` whose source `Z` is reduced and a
57	quasi-compact morphism `g : Y ⟶ X` whose source `Y` is reduced, `g` factors
58	through `i` if and only if the topological range of `g` is contained in the
59	topological range of `i`.
60	
61	This is the topological-range form of the universal property of closed
62	immersions; the kernel-inequality form is `IsClosedImmersion.lift` /
63	`IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac`.  The two forms are interchanged by the Galois
64	connection `vanishingIdeal ⊣ support` on ideal sheaves
65	(`IdealSheafData.gc` / `IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support`).
66	
67	This is **Substrate 1** of the Lane B Cross01 cocycle proof; see
68	`blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`,
69	`Theorem~\ref{thm:IsClosedImmersion_lift_iff_range_subset}`.
70	-/
71	theorem lift_iff_range_subset
72	    {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (i : Z ⟶ X) [IsClosedImmersion i] [IsReduced Z]
73	    (g : Y ⟶ X) [QuasiCompact g] [IsReduced Y] :
74	    (∃ h : Y ⟶ Z, h ≫ i = g) ↔ Set.range g.base ⊆ Set.range i.base := by
75	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
76	  · -- (⇒) Forward direction: range pushdown.
77	    rintro ⟨h, rfl⟩
78	    rintro _ ⟨y, rfl⟩
79	    refine ⟨h.base y, ?_⟩
80	    simp [Scheme.Hom.comp_base]
81	  · -- (⇐) Backward direction: the substantive content.
82	    intro hrange
83	    -- Helper: the kernel of a ring hom into a reduced ring is a radical ideal.
84	    have ker_isRadical : ∀ {R S : Type u} [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring S]
85	        [_root_.IsReduced S] (f : R →+* S), (RingHom.ker f).IsRadical := by
86	      intro R S _ _ _ f x hx
87	      obtain ⟨n, hxn⟩ := hx
88	      rw [RingHom.mem_ker] at hxn ⊢
89	      have h₁ : (f x) ^ n = 0 := by
90	        rw [← map_pow]; exact hxn
91	      exact eq_zero_of_pow_eq_zero h₁
92	    -- Step 1: For a closed immersion, the support of its kernel equals its range.
93	    -- (`support_ker` gives `closure (range)`; closed range collapses the closure.)
94	    have hi_range_eq : (i.ker.support : Set X) = Set.range i.base := by
95	      rw [Scheme.Hom.support_ker]
96	      exact i.isClosedEmbedding.isClosed_range.closure_eq
97	    -- Step 2: `g.ker.support ⊆ i.ker.support` (set-theoretic, via `closure_minimal`).
98	    have hsup_set : (g.ker.support : Set X) ⊆ (i.ker.support : Set X) := by
99	      rw [Scheme.Hom.support_ker]
100	      refine closure_minimal ?_ i.ker.support.isClosed
101	      exact hrange.trans hi_range_eq.ge
102	    -- Promote the set inclusion to the `Closeds X` ordering.
103	    have hsup : g.ker.support ≤ i.ker.support := hsup_set
104	    -- Step 3: `g.ker` is radical (because `Y` is reduced, so each `Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ U)` is reduced
105	    -- and the ring kernel is therefore radical, pointwise on affine opens).
106	    have hg_rad : g.ker.radical = g.ker := by
107	      refine le_antisymm ?_ (Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical _)
108	      intro U
109	      rw [Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, Scheme.Hom.ker_apply]
110	      exact ker_isRadical (g.app U.1).hom
111	    -- Step 4: `i.ker` is radical (same argument; closed immersions are quasi-compact).
112	    have hi_rad : i.ker.radical = i.ker := by
113	      refine le_antisymm ?_ (Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical _)
114	      intro U
115	      rw [Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, Scheme.Hom.ker_apply]
116	      exact ker_isRadical (i.app U.1).hom
117	    -- Step 5: thread the Galois connection `vanishingIdeal ⊣ support`.
118	    have hker : i.ker ≤ g.ker := by
119	      calc i.ker
120	          = i.ker.radical := hi_rad.symm
121	        _ = Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal i.ker.support :=
122	              Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support.symm
123	        _ ≤ Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal g.ker.support :=
124	              Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_antimono hsup
125	        _ = g.ker.radical := Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support
126	        _ = g.ker := hg_rad
127	    -- Step 6: feed the kernel inequality to Mathlib's `IsClosedImmersion.lift`.
128	    exact ⟨IsClosedImmersion.lift i g hker, IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac i g hker⟩
129	
130	end IsClosedImmersion
131	
132	/--
133	**Substrate 2 — `Away f ⊗_{k̄} GmRing k̄` is a domain.**
134	
135	For a non-zero homogeneous element `f` of positive degree `d` in the
136	`projectiveLineBar` grading on `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`, the tensor
137	`HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading k̄) f ⊗_{k̄} GmRing k̄`
138	is an integral domain.
139	
140	The proof identifies the tensor with the localization
141	`Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit (Away f))` via the
142	chain of natural isomorphisms in `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` §3:
143	* `Away f ⊗_{k̄} 𝕜[t] ≃ 𝕜[t][Away f]` (`MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv`),
144	* tensor base change with the localization
145	  `𝕜[t] → GmRing k̄` produces `Localization.Away (X ())`
146	  (`IsLocalization.Away.tensorRight`).
147	
148	`Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit (Away f))` is a domain because
149	it is the localization of `MvPolynomial Unit (Away f)` (a polynomial ring
150	over the domain `Away f`) at the powers of the non-zero generator `X ()`.

13	
14	For the Lane B (`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01`) iter-188 (III.c)
15	separated-locus structural setup, the substantive residual is a pair-
16	morphism factorization through the diagonal closed immersion. The
17	blueprint cites `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` as the
18	substrate; iter-188 verified this is NOT in Mathlib at b80f227. Two
19	questions:
20	
21	1. Is there a Mathlib variant of "morphism factors through closed
22	   immersion iff topological range subset" under any reasonable
23	   reduced-source hypothesis?
24	2. What is the cleanest project-side construction (with LOC), and what
25	   blocks the tensor-reducedness gap that also obstructs the other two
26	   off-target sorries (`gm_geomIrred`, `projGm_isReduced`)?

53	  The `lift_iff_range_subset` variant — equating factor-through with
54	  set-theoretic range containment under a reduced-source hypothesis —

56	  re-verified iter-189 via `lean_leanfinder`).

59	  would chain): the iter-189 search recovered the complete chain of

62	    (`.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/IdealSheaf/Basic.lean:689`):

74	    (same file, `:631-639`):
75	    `vanishingIdeal I.support = I.radical` — the Galois-connection
76	    pinning of "support determines radical".
77	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.gc`
78	    (same file, `:622-625`):
79	    `GaloisConnection (support ·) (vanishingIdeal ·)` (with the

82	    (`IdealSheaf/Subscheme.lean`):

92	  and the closed-immersion diagonal `hΔ`, leaving a single direct

108	### Decision 2: Tensor reducedness of `(Away (X_0·X_1)) ⊗_kbar (GmRing kbar)`
109

116	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Nilpotent/GeometricallyReduced.lean:47`):
117	    requires `IsReduced (AlgebraicClosure k ⊗_k A)`. The bridge
118	    "k alg-closed ⟹ IsReduced ⟹ IsGeometricallyReduced k" is NOT
119	    shipped (the file's own TODO confirms: "for every field extension
120	    K of k the K-algebra K ⊗[k] A is reduced" — not landed for the
121	    general field-extension case).
122	  - `Algebra.instIsReducedTensorProductOfIsAlgebraicOfIsGeometricallyReduced`
123	    (same file, `:52`): an instance for `IsReduced (K ⊗_k A)` when
124	    `K/k` algebraic AND `A` geometrically reduced over `k`. Doesn't
125	    apply directly because neither factor here is algebraic over kbar.
126	  - `Subalgebra.LinearDisjoint.isDomain_of_injective`
127	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/LinearDisjoint.lean`): the abstract route
128	    "tensor of two domains embedding into a common domain with
129	    linearly-disjoint ranges is a domain". Building the LinearDisjoint
130	    witness for our concrete factors would be its own ~30-50 LOC
131	    sub-task.
132	
133	- **The clean project path** (the analogist's recommended route):
134	  exploit Mathlib's **localization–polynomial tensor product
135	  iso machinery**. The relevant Mathlib lemmas, both shipped:
136	  - `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRightEquiv`
137	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Localization/BaseChange.lean`):
138	    `TensorProduct R A S ≃ₐ[S] Localization.Away ((algebraMap R S) r)`
139	    when `A = Localization.Away r` over `R`. Tensor of a localization
140	    with anything is a localization (in the tensored ring).
141	  - `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv`
142	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/TensorProduct/MvPolynomial.lean`):
143	    `TensorProduct R A (MvPolynomial σ R) ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial σ A`.
144	    Tensor with a polynomial ring over the base is just polynomials
145	    over the algebra.
146	
147	  Both factors of our tensor are localizations of polynomial rings
148	  over kbar:
149	  - `GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`
150	    — i.e., `kbar[t][1/t]`.

150	    — i.e., `kbar[t][1/t]`.
151	  - `Away (X_0·X_1) = HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projLineBarGrading) (X_0·X_1)`
152	    — a kbar-algebra that is a domain (already proven in
153	    `projectiveLineBar_isReduced` chain via the `val_injective`
154	    embedding into `Localization.Away (X_0·X_1)`).
155	
156	  Composition:
157	  1. `Away (X_0·X_1) ⊗_kbar kbar[t] ≃ MvPolynomial Unit (Away (X_0·X_1))`
158	     via `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv`.
159	  2. The localization `kbar[t] → kbar[t][1/t] = GmRing` corresponds via
160	     `IsScalarTower` + `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRightEquiv` to
161	     the localization at the same generator's image in the tensored
162	     polynomial ring.
163	  3. Result: `Away (X_0·X_1) ⊗_kbar GmRing ≃ Localization.Away (X () in MvPolynomial Unit (Away (X_0·X_1)))`.
164	  4. `MvPolynomial Unit (Away (X_0·X_1))` is a polynomial ring over a
165	     domain, hence a domain.
166	  5. Localization at powers of `X ()` (a non-zero divisor in the
167	     polynomial ring over a domain) preserves the domain property
168	     via `IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` +
169	     `powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors`
170	     + `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero` (already in the project's playbook,
171	     used in `projectiveLineBar_isReduced` L734-738).
172	
173	  Therefore `IsDomain (Away (X_0·X_1) ⊗_kbar GmRing)` follows, and
174	  `IsReduced` follows from `IsDomain` directly.
175	
176	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** — buildable at ~50-80 LOC.
177	  No new Mathlib infrastructure needed; everything is a chain of
178	  existing iso lemmas. The same pattern unifies the `gm_geomIrred` /
179	  `projGm_isReduced` closures (same generators, different chart degrees).
180	
181	### Decision 3: Where to land the substrate
182	
183	- **Recommended file layout**:
184	  - New helper file: `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Cross01Substrate.lean`
185	    importing `BareScheme`, `ChartIso`, `Points`. Contains:
186	    - `tensor_AwayGm_isDomain` (the tensor reducedness lemma, generic
187	      in the homogeneous generator's degree).
188	    - `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` (the substrate lemma,
189	      generic in the schemes).
190	  - Cross01 cocycle body lifts into `GmScaling.lean` (no file move
191	    needed — the substrate is reused from `Cross01Substrate.lean`).
192	
193	- **Why a new file vs inline in `GmScaling.lean`**:
194	  - The substrate is generic (no `gmScalingP1`-specific dependencies).
195	  - The substrate is reused by `gm_geomIrred` and `projGm_isReduced`
196	    (current `sorry`s in the SAME file), so a shared private namespace
197	    in `Cross01Substrate.lean` avoids duplication.
198	  - The substrate may be promotable to Mathlib later (the
199	    `lift_iff_range_subset` variant is a natural addition to
200	    `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.ClosedImmersion`).
201	
202	- **Verdict**: **BUILD_PROJECT_HELPER** in a new file `Cross01Substrate.lean`.
